description: Commercial letter in Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: ca. 1100 CE (Goitein's and Frenkel's estimate). No address. Sent to a "brother." Probably sent from Fustat to Alexandria (though it's not entirely clear how Goitein and Frenkel determined that the addressee is in Alexandria). The sender conveys his irritation that the addressee had not provided sufficient funds for a courier to pay the customs fees for the goods delivered. He provides many details about the processing of commodities at the port and customs house (ṣināʿa) in Fustat. Commodities mentioned: kohl, madder (fuwwa), safflower (ʿuṣfur), pepper, mastic, and maḥlab. People mentioned: Binyām; "all the merchants"; Muslim; a young man with a squint (ṣabiyy aḥwal); al-Maghribī; and Ibn Shekhanya. Also mentions an auction at Dār Ibn Nājiya. (Information in part from Frenkel and Goitein's attached notes; the document is also cited in Med Soc. I, p. 201 and several other places.)
description_he: מכתב מסחרי ביהודית-ערבית. מתוארך כ-1100. הכותב מביע כעסו שהנמען לא מסר מספיק כסף לשליח לתשלום מכס. מפרט את עיבוד הסחורות בנמל פוסטאט. סחורות: כחל, פואה, קרתם, פלפל, מסטיק ומחלב.
